title: rubus queenslandicus
intro: Rubus queenslandicus, also known as the Queensland rasp or wild raspberry, is a species of thorny shrub native to Australia. The plant is known for its edible fruits, leaves, and roots, which possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus queenslandicus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. Rubus queenslandicus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various herbal preparations.
